member.name: T:LLama.Abstractions.IContextParams
member.summary: The parameters for initializing a LLama context from a model.

member.name: P:LLama.Abstractions.IContextParams.ContextSize
member.summary: Model context size (n_ctx)

member.name: P:LLama.Abstractions.IContextParams.BatchSize
member.summary: maximum batch size that can be submitted at once (must be >=32 to use BLAS) (n_batch)

member.name: P:LLama.Abstractions.IContextParams.UBatchSize
member.summary: Physical batch size

member.name: P:LLama.Abstractions.IContextParams.SeqMax
member.summary: max number of sequences (i.e. distinct states for recurrent models)

member.name: P:LLama.Abstractions.IContextParams.Embeddings
member.summary: If true, extract embeddings (together with logits).

member.name: P:LLama.Abstractions.IContextParams.RopeFrequencyBase
member.summary: RoPE base frequency (null to fetch from the model)

member.name: P:LLama.Abstractions.IContextParams.RopeFrequencyScale
member.summary: RoPE frequency scaling factor (null to fetch from the model)

member.name: P:LLama.Abstractions.IContextParams.Encoding
member.summary: The encoding to use for models

member.name: P:LLama.Abstractions.IContextParams.Threads
member.summary: Number of threads (null = autodetect) (n_threads)

member.name: P:LLama.Abstractions.IContextParams.BatchThreads
member.summary: Number of threads to use for batch processing (null = autodetect) (n_threads)

member.name: P:LLama.Abstractions.IContextParams.YarnExtrapolationFactor
member.summary: YaRN extrapolation mix factor (null = from model)

member.name: P:LLama.Abstractions.IContextParams.YarnAttentionFactor
member.summary: YaRN magnitude scaling factor (null = from model)

member.name: P:LLama.Abstractions.IContextParams.YarnBetaFast
member.summary: YaRN low correction dim (null = from model)

member.name: P:LLama.Abstractions.IContextParams.YarnBetaSlow
member.summary: YaRN high correction dim (null = from model)

member.name: P:LLama.Abstractions.IContextParams.YarnOriginalContext
member.summary: YaRN original context length (null = from model)

member.name: P:LLama.Abstractions.IContextParams.YarnScalingType
member.summary: YaRN scaling method to use.

member.name: P:LLama.Abstractions.IContextParams.TypeK
member.summary: Override the type of the K cache

member.name: P:LLama.Abstractions.IContextParams.TypeV
member.summary: Override the type of the V cache

member.name: P:LLama.Abstractions.IContextParams.NoKqvOffload
member.summary: Whether to disable offloading the KQV cache to the GPU

member.name: P:LLama.Abstractions.IContextParams.FlashAttention
member.summary: Whether to use flash attention

member.name: P:LLama.Abstractions.IContextParams.DefragThreshold
member.summary: defragment the KV cache if holes/size > defrag_threshold, Set to <= 0 to disable (default)

member.name: P:LLama.Abstractions.IContextParams.PoolingType
member.summary: How to pool (sum) embedding results by sequence id (ignored if no pooling layer)

member.name: P:LLama.Abstractions.IContextParams.AttentionType
member.summary: Attention type to use for embeddings

member.name: P:LLama.Abstractions.IContextParams.OpOffload
member.summary: Offload host tensor operations to device

member.name: P:LLama.Abstractions.IContextParams.KVUnified
member.summary:
use a unified buffer across the input sequences when computing the attention. try to disable when n_seq_max > 1 for improved performance when the sequences do not share a large prefix
ref: https://github.com/ggml-org/llama.cpp/pull/14363

member.name: P:LLama.Abstractions.IContextParams.SwaFull
member.summary: Use full-size SWA cache (https://github.com/ggml-org/llama.cpp/pull/13194#issuecomment-2868343055)
member.remarks: Setting to false when n_seq_max > 1 can cause bad performance in some cases ref: https://github.com/ggml-org/llama.cpp/pull/13845#issuecomment-2924800573

None The GPU that is used for the entire mode.

Layer Ignored.

member.name: M:LLama.Batched.Conversation.Fork
member.summary: Create a copy of the current conversation
member.remarks: The copy shares internal state, so consumes very little extra memory.

member.name: M:LLama.Batched.Conversation.GetSampleIndex(System.Int32)
member.summary: Get the index in the context which each token can be sampled from, the return value of this function get be used to retrieve logits () or to sample a token (.
member.param:
How far from the end of the previous prompt should logits be sampled. Any value other than 0 requires allLogits to have been set during prompting.
For example if 5 tokens were supplied in the last prompt call:
The logits of the first token can be accessed with 4
The logits of the second token can be accessed with 3
The logits of the third token can be accessed with 2
The logits of the fourth token can be accessed with 1
The logits of the fifth token can be accessed with 0
member.exception: Thrown if this conversation was not prompted before the previous call to infer
Thrown if Infer() must be called on the executor

member.name: M:LLama.Batched.Conversation.Sample(System.Int32)
member.summary: Get the logits from this conversation, ready for sampling
member.param: How far from the end of the previous prompt should logits be sampled. Any value other than 0 requires allLogits to have been set during prompting
member.exception: Thrown if this conversation was not prompted before the previous call to infer
Thrown if Infer() must be called on the executor

member.name: M:LLama.Extensions.SpanNormalizationExtensions.PNormalization(System.Single[],System.Int32)
member.summary:
In-place apply p-normalization. https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Norm_(mathematics)#p-norm
For p = 1, this is taxicab normalization
For p = 2, this is euclidean normalization
As p => infinity, this approaches infinity norm or maximum norm
member.returns: The same array

member.name: M:LLama.Extensions.SpanNormalizationExtensions.PNormalization(System.Span{System.Single},System.Int32)
member.summary:
In-place apply p-normalization. https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Norm_(mathematics)#p-norm
For p = 1, this is taxicab normalization
For p = 2, this is euclidean normalization
As p => infinity, this approaches infinity norm or maximum norm
member.returns: The same span

member.summary: Keywords that you want to remove from the response. This property is used for JSON serialization.

member.summary: Maximum length of the keywords. This property is used for JSON serialization.

member.name: P:LLama.LLamaTransforms.KeywordTextOutputStreamTransform.RemoveAllMatchedTokens
member.summary: If set to true, when getting a matched keyword, all the related tokens will be removed. Otherwise only the part of keyword will be removed. This property is used for JSON serialization.

member.name: M:LLama.LLamaTransforms.KeywordTextOutputStreamTransform.#ctor(System.Collections.Generic.HashSet{System.String},System.Int32,System.Boolean)
member.summary: JSON constructor.

member.name: M:LLama.LLamaTransforms.KeywordTextOutputStreamTransform.#ctor(System.Collections.Generic.IEnumerable{System.String},System.Int32,System.Boolean)
member.param: Keywords that you want to remove from the response.

The extra length when searching for the keyword. For example, if your only keyword is "highlight", maybe the token you get is "\r\nhighligt". In this condition, if redundancyLength=0, the token cannot be successfully matched because the length of "\r\nhighligt" (10) has already exceeded the maximum length of the keywords (8). On the contrary, setting redundancyLengyh >= 2 leads to successful match. The larger the redundancyLength is, the lower the processing speed. But as an experience, it won't introduce too much performance impact when redundancyLength <= 5
If set to true, when getting a matched keyword, all the related tokens will be removed. Otherwise only the part of keyword will be removed.

member.name: T:LLama.Native.GGMLType
member.summary: Possible GGML quantisation types

member.name: F:LLama.Native.GGMLType.GGML_TYPE_F32
member.summary: Full 32 bit float

member.name: F:LLama.Native.GGMLType.GGML_TYPE_F16
member.summary: 16 bit float

member.name: F:LLama.Native.GGMLType.GGML_TYPE_Q4_0
member.summary: 4 bit float

member.name: F:LLama.Native.GGMLType.GGML_TYPE_Q4_1
member.summary: 4 bit float

member.name: F:LLama.Native.GGMLType.GGML_TYPE_Q5_0
member.summary: 5 bit float

member.name: F:LLama.Native.GGMLType.GGML_TYPE_Q5_1
member.summary: 5 bit float

member.name: F:LLama.Native.GGMLType.GGML_TYPE_Q8_0
member.summary: 8 bit float

member.name: F:LLama.Native.GGMLType.GGML_TYPE_Q8_1
member.summary: 8 bit float

member.name: F:LLama.Native.GGMLType.GGML_TYPE_Q2_K
member.summary: "type-1" 2-bit quantization in super-blocks containing 16 blocks, each block having 16 weight. Block scales and mins are quantized with 4 bits. This ends up effectively using 2.5625 bits per weight (bpw)

member.name: F:LLama.Native.GGMLType.GGML_TYPE_Q3_K
member.summary: "type-0" 3-bit quantization in super-blocks containing 16 blocks, each block having 16 weights. Scales are quantized with 6 bits. This end up using 3.4375 bpw.

member.name: F:LLama.Native.GGMLType.GGML_TYPE_Q4_K
member.summary: "type-1" 4-bit quantization in super-blocks containing 8 blocks, each block having 32 weights. Scales and mins are quantized with 6 bits. This ends up using 4.5 bpw.

member.name: F:LLama.Native.GGMLType.GGML_TYPE_Q5_K
member.summary: "type-1" 5-bit quantization. Same super-block structure as GGML_TYPE_Q4_K resulting in 5.5 bpw

member.name: F:LLama.Native.GGMLType.GGML_TYPE_Q6_K
member.summary: "type-0" 6-bit quantization. Super-blocks with 16 blocks, each block having 16 weights. Scales are quantized with 8 bits. This ends up using 6.5625 bpw

member.name: F:LLama.Native.GGMLType.GGML_TYPE_Q8_K
member.summary: "type-0" 8-bit quantization. Only used for quantizing intermediate results. The difference to the existing Q8_0 is that the block size is 256. All 2-6 bit dot products are implemented for this quantization type.

member.name: F:LLama.Native.GGMLType.GGML_TYPE_I8
member.summary: Integer, 8 bit

member.name: F:LLama.Native.GGMLType.GGML_TYPE_I16
member.summary: Integer, 16 bit

member.name: F:LLama.Native.GGMLType.GGML_TYPE_I32
member.summary: Integer, 32 bit

member.name: F:LLama.Native.GGMLType.GGML_TYPE_COUNT
member.summary: The value of this entry is the count of the number of possible quant types.

member.summary: use full-size SWA cache (https://github.com/ggml-org/llama.cpp/pull/13194#issuecomment-2868343055) NOTE: setting to false when n_seq_max > 1 can cause bad performance in some cases ref: https://github.com/ggml-org/llama.cpp/pull/13845#issuecomment-2924800573

member.name: P:LLama.Native.LLamaContextParams.kv_unified
member.summary:
use a unified buffer across the input sequences when computing the attention. try to disable when n_seq_max > 1 for improved performance when the sequences do not share a large prefix
ref: https://github.com/ggml-org/llama.cpp/pull/14363

Try to load the native library with the current configurations, but do not actually set it to .
You can still modify the configuration after this calling but only before any call from .

Initialize the llama + ggml backend. Call once at the start of the program.
This is private because LLamaSharp automatically calls it, and it's only valid to call it once!

member.summary: Saves the specified sequence as a file on specified filepath. Can later be loaded via

member.summary: Set abort callback

member.name: M:LLama.Native.NativeApi.llama_get_embeddings(LLama.Native.SafeLLamaContextHandle)
member.summary: Get all output token embeddings. When pooling_type == LLAMA_POOLING_TYPE_NONE or when using a generative model, the embeddings for which llama_batch.logits[i] != 0 are stored contiguously in the order they have appeared in the batch. shape: [n_outputs*n_embd] Otherwise, returns an empty span.

member.name: M:LLama.Native.NativeApi.llama_chat_apply_template(System.Byte*,LLama.Native.LLamaChatMessage*,System.UIntPtr,System.Boolean,System.Byte*,System.Int32)
member.summary:
Apply chat template. Inspired by hf apply_chat_template() on python.
NOTE: This function does not use a jinja parser. It only support a pre-defined list of template. See more: https://github.com/ggml-org/llama.cpp/wiki/Templates-supported-by-llama_chat_apply_template
member.param: A Jinja template to use for this chat.
Pointer to a list of multiple llama_chat_message
Number of llama_chat_message in this chat
Whether to end the prompt with the token(s) that indicate the start of an assistant message.
A buffer to hold the output formatted prompt. The recommended alloc size is 2 * (total number of characters of all messages)
The size of the allocated buffer
member.returns: The total number of bytes of the formatted prompt. If is it larger than the size of buffer, you may need to re-alloc it and then re-apply the template.

member.name: M:LLama.Native.NativeApi.llama_chat_builtin_templates(System.Char**,System.UIntPtr)
member.summary: Get list of built-in chat templates

member.summary: Print out timing information for this context

member.name: M:LLama.Native.NativeApi.llama_print_system_info
member.summary: Print system information

member.name: M:LLama.Native.NativeApi.llama_token_to_piece(LLama.Native.SafeLlamaModelHandle.Vocabulary,LLama.Native.LLamaToken,System.Span{System.Byte},System.Int32,System.Boolean)
member.summary: Convert a single token into text
member.param: buffer to write string into
User can skip up to 'lstrip' leading spaces before copying (useful when encoding/decoding multiple tokens with 'add_space_prefix')
If true, special tokens are rendered in the output
member.returns: The length written, or if the buffer is too small a negative that indicates the length required

member.name: M:LLama.Native.NativeApi.llama_tokenize(LLama.Native.LLamaVocabNative*,System.Byte*,System.Int32,LLama.Native.LLamaToken*,System.Int32,System.Boolean,System.Boolean)
member.summary: Convert text into tokens
member.param: The tokens pointer must be large enough to hold the resulting tokens.
add_special Allow to add BOS and EOS tokens if model is configured to do so.
Allow tokenizing special and/or control tokens which otherwise are not exposed and treated as plaintext. Does not insert a leading space.
member.returns: Returns the number of tokens on success, no more than n_max_tokens. Returns a negative number on failure - the number of tokens that would have been returned. Returns INT32_MIN on overflow (e.g., tokenization result size exceeds int32_t limit)

member.name: M:LLama.Native.NativeApi.llama_detokenize(LLama.Native.LLamaVocabNative*,LLama.Native.LLamaToken*,System.Int32,System.Byte*,System.Int32,System.Boolean,System.Boolean)
member.summary: Convert the provided tokens into text (inverse of llama_tokenize()).
member.param: The char pointer must be large enough to hold the resulting text.
remove_special Allow to remove BOS and EOS tokens if model is configured to do so.
unparse_special If true, special tokens are rendered in the output.
member.returns: Returns the number of chars/bytes on success, no more than textLengthMax. Returns a negative number on failure - the number of chars/bytes that would have been returned.

member.name: M:LLama.Native.NativeApi.llama_log_set(LLama.Native.NativeLogConfig.LLamaLogCallback)
member.summary: Register a callback to receive llama log messages

member.name: M:LLama.Native.NativeApi.llama_batch_init(System.Int32,System.Int32,System.Int32)
member.summary: Allocates a batch of tokens on the heap Each token can be assigned up to n_seq_max sequence ids The batch has to be freed with llama_batch_free() If embd != 0, llama_batch.embd will be allocated with size of n_tokens * embd * sizeof(float) Otherwise, llama_batch.token will be allocated to store n_tokens llama_token The rest of the llama_batch members are allocated with size n_tokens All members are left uninitialized
member.param: Each token can be assigned up to n_seq_max sequence ids

member.name: M:LLama.Native.NativeApi.llama_batch_free(LLama.Native.LLamaNativeBatch)
member.summary: Frees a batch of tokens allocated with llama_batch_init()

member.name: M:LLama.Native.NativeApi.llama_set_adapter_cvec(LLama.Native.SafeLLamaContextHandle,System.Single*,System.UIntPtr,System.Int32,System.Int32,System.Int32)
member.summary: Apply a loaded control vector to a llama_context, or if data is NULL, clear the currently loaded vector. n_embd should be the size of a single layer's control, and data should point to an n_embd x n_layers buffer starting from layer 1. il_start and il_end are the layer range the vector should apply to (both inclusive) See llama_control_vector_load in common to load a control vector.

member.name: M:LLama.Native.NativeApi.llama_split_path_native(System.Byte*,System.UIntPtr,System.Byte*,System.Int32,System.Int32)
member.summary: Build a split GGUF final path for this chunk. llama_split_path(split_path, sizeof(split_path), "/models/ggml-model-q4_0", 2, 4) => split_path = "/models/ggml-model-q4_0-00002-of-00004.gguf"

member.name: M:LLama.Native.NativeApi.llama_params_fit(System.String,LLama.Native.LLamaModelParams@,LLama.Native.LLamaContextParams@,System.Single*,LLama.Native.LLamaModelTensorBufferOverride*,System.IntPtr*,System.UInt32,System.Int32)
member.summary: Fits mparams and cparams to free device memory (assumes system memory is unlimited) - returns true if the parameters could be successfully modified to fit device memory - this function is NOT thread safe because it modifies the global llama logger state - only parameters that have the same value as in llama_default_model_params are modified with the exception of the context size which is modified if and only if equal to 0
member.param: Writable buffer for tensor split, needs at least llama_max_devices elements
Writable buffer for overrides, needs at least llama_max_tensor_buft_overrides elements
Margins of memory to leave per device in bytes
Minimum context size to set when trying to reduce memory use
Minimum log level to print during fitting, lower levels go to debug log

member.summary:
Integer division of the positions by factor of `d > 1`
p0 < 0 : [0, p1]
p1 < 0 : [p0, inf)

member.name: M:LLama.Native.NativeApi.llama_memory_seq_pos_min(System.IntPtr,LLama.Native.LLamaSeqId)
member.summary: Returns the smallest position present in the memory for the specified sequence. This is typically non-zero only for SWA caches. Note that all positions in the range [pos_min, pos_max] are guaranteed to be present in the memory. Return -1 if the sequence is empty.

member.name: M:LLama.Native.NativeApi.llama_memory_seq_pos_max(System.IntPtr,LLama.Native.LLamaSeqId)
member.summary: Returns the largest position present in the memory for the specified sequence. Note that all positions in the range [pos_min, pos_max] are guaranteed to be present in the memory. Return -1 if the sequence is empty.

member.name: M:LLama.Native.SafeLLamaContextHandle.llama_decode(LLama.Native.SafeLLamaContextHandle,LLama.Native.LLamaNativeBatch)
member.summary: Process a batch of tokens. Requires the context to have a memory. For encode-decoder contexts, processes the batch using the decoder. Positive return values does not mean a fatal error, but rather a warning. Upon fatal-error or abort, the ubatches that managed to be been processed will remain in the memory state of the context To handle this correctly, query the memory state using llama_memory_seq_pos_min() and llama_memory_seq_pos_max() Upon other return values, the memory state is restored to the state before this call 0 - success 1 - could not find a memory slot for the batch (try reducing the size of the batch or increase the context) 2 - aborted (processed ubatches will remain in the context's memory) -1 - invalid input batch < -1 - fatal error (processed ubatches will remain in the context's memory)

member.name: M:LLama.Native.SafeLLamaContextHandle.llama_encode(LLama.Native.SafeLLamaContextHandle,LLama.Native.LLamaNativeBatch)
member.summary: Process a batch of tokens. In contrast to llama_decode() - this call does not use KV cache. For encode-decoder contexts, processes the batch using the encoder. Can store the encoder output internally for later use by the decoder's cross-attention layers.
member.returns:
0 = success
< 0 = error

member.name: M:LLama.Native.SafeLLamaContextHandle.llama_set_n_threads(LLama.Native.SafeLLamaContextHandle,System.Int32,System.Int32)
member.summary: Set the number of threads used for decoding
member.param: n_threads is the number of threads used for generation (single token)
n_threads_batch is the number of threads used for prompt and batch processing (multiple tokens)

member.name: M:LLama.Native.SafeLLamaContextHandle.GetLogits(System.Int32)
member.summary:
Token logits obtained from the last call to llama_decode. The logits for the last token are stored in the last row. Only tokens with `logits = true` requested are present.
Can be mutated in order to change the probabilities of the next token.
Rows: n_tokens
Cols: n_vocab
member.param:
The amount of tokens whose logits should be retrieved, in [numTokens X n_vocab] format.
Tokens' order is based on their order in the LlamaBatch (so, first tokens are first, etc).
This is helpful when requesting logits for many tokens in a sequence, or want to decode multiple sequences in one go.

member.name: M:LLama.Native.SafeLLamaContextHandle.GetLogitsIth(System.Int32)
member.summary: Logits for the ith token. Equivalent to: llama_get_logits(ctx) + i*n_vocab

member.name: M:LLama.Native.SafeLLamaContextHandle.GetEmbeddingsIth(LLama.Native.LLamaPos)
member.summary: Get the embeddings for the ith sequence. Equivalent to: llama_get_embeddings(ctx) + ctx->output_ids[i]*n_embd
member.returns: A pointer to the first float in an embedding, length = ctx.EmbeddingSize

member.name: M:LLama.Native.SafeLLamaContextHandle.GetEmbeddingsSeq(LLama.Native.LLamaSeqId)
member.summary: Get the embeddings for the a specific sequence. Equivalent to: llama_get_embeddings(ctx) + ctx->output_ids[i]*n_embd
member.returns: A pointer to the first float in an embedding, length = ctx.EmbeddingSize

member.summary: Convert the given text into tokens
member.param: The text to tokenize
Whether the "BOS" token should be added
Encoding to use for the text
Allow tokenizing special and/or control tokens which otherwise are not exposed and treated as plaintext.

member.name: M:LLama.Native.SafeLLamaContextHandle.TokenToSpan(LLama.Native.LLamaToken,System.Span{System.Byte})
member.summary: Convert a single llama token into bytes
member.param: Token to decode
A span to attempt to write into. If this is too small nothing will be written
member.returns: The size of this token. **nothing will be written** if this is larger than `dest`

member.name: F:LLama.Native.SafeLLamaContextHandle.GlobalInferenceLock
member.summary:
This object exists to ensure there is only ever 1 inference running at a time. This is a workaround for thread safety issues in llama.cpp itself. Most notably CUDA, which seems to use some global singleton resources and will crash if multiple inferences are run (even against different models).
For more information see these issues: - https://github.com/SciSharp/LLamaSharp/issues/596 - https://github.com/ggerganov/llama.cpp/issues/3960
If these are ever resolved this lock can probably be removed.

member.name: M:LLama.Native.SafeLLamaContextHandle.Synchronize
member.summary: Wait until all computations are finished. This is automatically done when using any of the functions to obtain computation results and is not necessary to call it explicitly in most cases.

member.name: M:LLama.Native.SafeLLamaContextHandle.Encode(LLama.Native.LLamaBatch)
member.summary: Processes a batch of tokens with the encoder part of the encoder-decoder model. Stores the encoder output internally for later use by the decoder cross-attention layers.
member.returns:
0 = success
< 0 = error (the memory state is restored to the state before this call)

member.name: M:LLama.Native.SafeLLamaContextHandle.Decode(LLama.Native.LLamaBatch)
member.summary: Process a batch of tokens. Requires the context to have a memory. For encode-decoder contexts, processes the batch using the decoder. Positive return values does not mean a fatal error, but rather a warning. Upon fatal-error or abort, the ubatches that managed to be been processed will remain in the memory state of the context To handle this correctly, query the memory state using llama_memory_seq_pos_min() and llama_memory_seq_pos_max() Upon other return values, the memory state is restored to the state before this call 0 - success 1 - could not find a memory slot for the batch (try reducing the size of the batch or increase the context) 2 - aborted (processed ubatches will remain in the context's memory) -1 - invalid input batch < -1 - fatal error (processed ubatches will remain in the context's memory)

member.name: M:LLama.Native.SafeLLamaContextHandle.Decode(System.Collections.Generic.List{LLama.Native.LLamaToken},LLama.Native.LLamaSeqId,LLama.Native.LLamaBatch,System.Int32@)
member.summary: Decode a set of tokens in batch-size chunks.
member.returns: A tuple, containing the decode result and the number of tokens that have not been decoded yet.

member.name: M:LLama.Native.SafeLLamaContextHandle.Decode(LLama.Native.LLamaBatchEmbeddings)
member.summary: Process a batch of tokens. Requires the context to have a memory. For encode-decoder contexts, processes the batch using the decoder. Positive return values does not mean a fatal error, but rather a warning. Upon fatal-error or abort, the ubatches that managed to be been processed will remain in the memory state of the context To handle this correctly, query the memory state using llama_memory_seq_pos_min() and llama_memory_seq_pos_max() Upon other return values, the memory state is restored to the state before this call 0 - success 1 - could not find a memory slot for the batch (try reducing the size of the batch or increase the context) 2 - aborted (processed ubatches will remain in the context's memory) -1 - invalid input batch < -1 - fatal error (processed ubatches will remain in the context's memory)

member.name: M:LLama.Native.SafeLLamaSamplerChainHandle.AddMirostat1Sampler(System.Int32,System.UInt32,System.Single,System.Single,System.Int32)
member.summary: Mirostat 1.0 algorithm described in the paper https://arxiv.org/abs/2007.14966. Uses tokens instead of words.
member.param: The target cross-entropy (or surprise) value you want to achieve for the generated text. A higher value corresponds to more surprising or less predictable text, while a lower value corresponds to less surprising or more predictable text.

The learning rate used to update `mu` based on the error between the target and observed surprisal of the sampled word. A larger learning rate will cause `mu` to be updated more quickly, while a smaller learning rate will result in slower updates.

The number of tokens considered in the estimation of `s_hat`. This is an arbitrary value that is used to calculate `s_hat`, which in turn helps to calculate the value of `k`. In the paper, they use `m = 100`, but you can experiment with different values to see how it affects the performance of the algorithm.

member.name: M:LLama.Native.SafeLLamaSamplerChainHandle.AddMirostat2Sampler(System.UInt32,System.Single,System.Single)
member.summary: Mirostat 2.0 algorithm described in the paper https://arxiv.org/abs/2007.14966. Uses tokens instead of words.
member.param: The target cross-entropy (or surprise) value you want to achieve for the generated text. A higher value corresponds to more surprising or less predictable text, while a lower value corresponds to less surprising or more predictable text.

The learning rate used to update `mu` based on the error between the target and observed surprisal of the sampled word. A larger learning rate will cause `mu` to be updated more quickly, while a smaller learning rate will result in slower updates.

member.name: M:LLama.Native.SafeLLamaSamplerChainHandle.AddTopK(System.Int32)
member.summary: Top-K sampling described in academic paper "The Curious Case of Neural Text Degeneration" https://arxiv.org/abs/1904.09751

member.summary: Dynamic temperature implementation (a.k.a. entropy) described in the paper https://arxiv.org/abs/2309.02772.

member.name: M:LLama.Native.SafeLLamaSamplerChainHandle.AddXTC(System.Single,System.Single,System.Int32,System.UInt32)
member.summary: XTC sampler as described in https://github.com/oobabooga/text-generation-webui/pull/6335

member.name: M:LLama.Native.SafeLLamaSamplerChainHandle.AddFillInMiddleInfill(LLama.Native.SafeLlamaModelHandle)
member.summary:
This sampler is meant to be used for fill-in-the-middle infilling, after top_k + top_p sampling
1. if the sum of the EOG probs times the number of candidates is higher than the sum of the other probs -> pick EOG
2. combine probs of tokens that have the same prefix
example:
- before:
"abc": 0.5
"abcd": 0.2
"abcde": 0.1
"dummy": 0.1
- after:
"abc": 0.8
"dummy": 0.1
3. discard non-EOG tokens with low prob
4. if no tokens are left -> pick EOT

member.summary: Create a sampler using lazy grammar sampling: https://github.com/ggerganov/llama.cpp/pull/9639
member.param: Grammar in GBNF form
Root rule of the grammar
A list of patterns that will trigger the grammar sampler. Pattern will be matched from the start of the generation output, and grammar sampler will be fed content starting from its first match group.
A list of tokens that will trigger the grammar sampler. Grammar sampler will be fed content starting from the trigger token included..

member.summary:
Create a sampler that applies various repetition penalties.
Avoid using on the full vocabulary as searching for repeated tokens can become slow. For example, apply top-k or top-p sampling first.

Repetition penalty
Frequency penalty
Presence penalty

member.name: M:LLama.Native.SafeLLamaSamplerChainHandle.AddDry(LLama.Native.SafeLlamaModelHandle,System.ReadOnlySpan{System.String},System.Single,System.Single,System.Int32,System.Int32)
member.summary: DRY sampler, designed by p-e-w, as described in: https://github.com/oobabooga/text-generation-webui/pull/5677. Porting Koboldcpp implementation authored by pi6am: https://github.com/LostRuins/koboldcpp/pull/982
member.param: The model this sampler will be used with
penalty multiplier, 0.0 = disabled
exponential base
repeated sequences longer than this are penalized
how many tokens to scan for repetitions (0 = entire context)

member.name: M:LLama.Native.SafeLLamaSamplerChainHandle.AddAdaptiveP(System.Single,System.Single,System.UInt32)
member.summary:
Adaptive-p: select tokens near a configurable target probability over time.
the adaptive-p sampler transforms the token probability distribution to favor tokens that fall near a user-configurable probability target.
internally, the sampler maintains an exponential moving average of the *ORIGINAL* probabilities of selected tokens at each sampling step. it uses this EMA to compute an adapted target probability at each sampling step, thus maintaining the desired target probability over time.
adaptive-p selects a token ID rather than just mutating candidates, so it must be last in the sampler chain (like mirostat, dist, greedy).
only mild truncation before this sampler is recommended. we suggest applying min-p before adaptive-p as the only other active sampler in the chain.
ref: https://github.com/ggml-org/llama.cpp/pull/17927
member.param: select tokens near this probability (valid range 0.0 to 1.0; negative = disabled)
EMA decay for adaptation; history ≈ 1/(1-decay) tokens (valid range 0.0 - 0.99)
RNG seed

member.summary: Repetition penalty, as described in https://arxiv.org/abs/1909.05858

member.name: P:LLama.Sampling.DefaultSamplingPipeline.FrequencyPenalty
member.summary:
Frequency penalty as described by OpenAI: https://platform.openai.com/docs/api-reference/chat/create
Number between -2.0 and 2.0. Positive values penalize new tokens based on their existing frequency in the text so far, decreasing the model's likelihood to repeat the same line verbatim.

member.name: P:LLama.Sampling.DefaultSamplingPipeline.PresencePenalty
member.summary:
Presence penalty as described by OpenAI: https://platform.openai.com/docs/api-reference/chat/create
Number between -2.0 and 2.0. Positive values penalize new tokens based on whether they appear in the text so far, increasing the model's likelihood to talk about new topics.

member.name: P:LLama.Sampling.DefaultSamplingPipeline.PenaltyCount
member.summary: How many tokens should be considered for penalties

member.summary: Whether the newline token should be protected from being modified by penalty
